questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In reducing a given equation to the standard forms $(\sin x=\sin \alpha$ etc $)$ we apply several trigonometric or algebraic transformations. As a result of which the canonical form finally obtained may not be equivalent to the original equation resulting either in loss of solutions or in the appearance of fake solutionsQuestion: The solution set of the equation $\sqrt{5-2 \sin x}=6 \sin x-1$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\sin \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{t} ; \mathrm{t} \in[-1,1]$
$\sqrt{5-2 t}=6 t-1 \geq 0 \quad$ i.e. $\quad t \geq \frac{1}{6}$
So, $t \in\left[\frac{1}{6}, 1\right]$
Now squaring, $\sqrt{5-2 \mathrm{t}}=(6 \mathrm{t}-1), \mathrm{t}=\frac{1}{2},-\frac{2}{9}, \mathrm{t}=-\frac{2}{9}$ not possible $\left(\because \mathrm{t} \in\left[\frac{1}{6}, 1\right]\right)$
So, $t=\frac{1}{2}, \sin x=\frac{1}{2}=\sin \frac{\pi}{6}, x=n \pi+(-1)^n \frac{\pi}{6}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a, b, c, d \in R$. Then the cubic equation of the type $a x^3+b x^2+c x+d=0$ has either one root real or all three roots are real. But in case of trigonometric equations of the type $a \sin ^3 x+b \sin ^2 x+c \sin x+d=0$ can possess several solutions depending upon the domain of $x$. To solve an equation of the type $a \cos \theta+b \sin \theta=c$. The equation can be written as $\cos (\theta-\alpha)=c / \sqrt{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}$. The solution is $\theta=2 \mathrm{n} \pi+\alpha \pm \beta$, where tan $\alpha=b / a, \cos$ $\beta=c / \sqrt{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}$Question: In the interval $[-\pi / 4, \pi / 2]$, the equation, $\cos 4 x+\frac{10 \tan x}{1+\tan ^2 x}=3$ has
questions.solution:
$3=\cos 4 x+\frac{10 \tan x}{1+\tan ^2 x}=\cos 4 x+5 \sin 2 x$ i.e $3=1-2 \sin ^2 2 x+5 \sin 2 x$
i.e $\sin 2 x=\frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore \quad 2 x=\frac{\pi}{6}, \frac{5 \pi}{6}$
Thus there are two solutions.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a, b, c, d \in R$. Then the cubic equation of the type $a x^3+b x^2+c x+d=0$ has either one root real or all three roots are real. But in case of trigonometric equations of the type $a \sin ^3 x+b \sin ^2 x+c \sin x+d=0$ can possess several solutions depending upon the domain of $x$. To solve an equation of the type $a \cos \theta+b \sin \theta=c$. The equation can be written as $\cos (\theta-\alpha)=c / \sqrt{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}$. The solution is $\theta=2 \mathrm{n} \pi+\alpha \pm \beta$, where tan $\alpha=b / a, \cos$ $\beta=c / \sqrt{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}$Question: $|\tan x|=\tan x+\frac{1}{\cos x}(0 \leq x \leq 2 \pi)$ has
questions.solution:
(i) When $\tan x \geq 0$, then the equation becomes $\tan x=\tan x+\frac{1}{\cos x}$ i.e $\frac{1}{\cos x}=0$ (not possible)
(ii) When $\tan x \lt 0$, then the equation becomes $-\tan x=\tan x+\frac{1}{\cos x}$ i.e $\sin x=-\frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore \quad x=\frac{11 \pi}{6}$ is the only solution.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $a, b, c, d \in R$. Then the cubic equation of the type $a x^3+b x^2+c x+d=0$ has either one root real or all three roots are real. But in case of trigonometric equations of the type $a \sin ^3 x+b \sin ^2 x+c \sin x+d=0$ can possess several solutions depending upon the domain of $x$. To solve an equation of the type $a \cos \theta+b \sin \theta=c$. The equation can be written as $\cos (\theta-\alpha)=c / \sqrt{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}$. The solution is $\theta=2 \mathrm{n} \pi+\alpha \pm \beta$, where tan $\alpha=b / a, \cos$ $\beta=c / \sqrt{\left(a^2+b^2\right)}$Question: On the domain $[-\pi, \pi]$ the equation $4 \sin ^3 x+2 \sin ^2 x-2 \sin x-1=0$ possess
questions.solution:
$4 \sin ^3 x+2 \sin ^2 x-2 \sin x-1=(2 \sin x+1)\left(2 \sin ^2 x-1\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad \sin x=-\frac{1}{2}, \pm \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
$\therefore \quad$ There are 6 solutions.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In reducing a given equation to the standard forms $(\sin x=\sin \alpha$ etc $)$ we apply several trigonometric or algebraic transformations. As a result of which the canonical form finally obtained may not be equivalent to the original equation resulting either in loss of solutions or in the appearance of fake solutions The equation $2 \cot 2 x-3 \cot 3 x=\tan 2 x$ has
questions.solution: $2 \frac{\cos 2 x}{\sin 2 x}-3 \frac{\cos 3 x}{\sin 3 x}=\frac{\sin 2 x}{\cos 2 x}$ $2 \frac{\left(2 \cos ^2 x-1\right)}{2 \sin x \cos x}-3 \frac{4 \cos ^3 x-3 \cos x}{3 \sin x-4 \sin ^3 x}=\frac{2 \sin x \cos x}{2 \cos ^2 x-1}$ Now Let $\cos ^2 \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{t}$ in above i.e. $t \in(0,1)-\left\{\frac{1}{2}\right\}$ Now we have $2 t-1-3 t \frac{4 t-3}{4 t-1}=\frac{2 t(1-t)}{2 t-1}$ Solving $\mathrm{t}= \pm 1$ but $\mathrm{t} \in(0,1)-\left\{\frac{1}{2}\right\}$ So there is no $t$, so there is no $x$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: While finding the sine of a contain angle $x$, an absent minded professor failed to notice that his calculator was not in the correct angular mode. However he was lucky to get right answer. The two least positive value of $x$ for which the sine of $x$ degree is the same as the sine of $x$ radian found by him as $\frac{m \pi}{n-\pi}$ and $\frac{p \pi}{q+\pi}$, where $m, n, p$ and $q$ are positive integers. Suppose $\frac{m n}{p q}$ be denoted by quantity ' $L$ 'Question: If $x$ is measured in radians and $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\sqrt{A x^2+B x}-C x\right)=2$, the value of $\frac{B C}{A}$ equals $(A, B, C \in R)$
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\left(A-C^2\right) x^2+B x}{x\left(\sqrt{A+\frac{B}{x}}+C\right)}=2$
For existence of limit $A=C^2$
Hence, $C=\sqrt{A}, \frac{B}{\sqrt{A}+C}=2$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{C}}=4$
For $C=-\sqrt{A}$ limit does not exist
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: While finding the sine of a contain angle $x$, an absent minded professor failed to notice that his calculator was not in the correct angular mode. However he was lucky to get right answer. The two least positive value of $x$ for which the sine of $x$ degree is the same as the sine of $x$ radian found by him as $\frac{m \pi}{n-\pi}$ and $\frac{p \pi}{q+\pi}$, where $m, n, p$ and $q$ are positive integers. Suppose $\frac{m n}{p q}$ be denoted by quantity ' $L$ 'Question: The value of $(m+n+p+q)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \sin x=\sin \frac{(\pi x)}{180} \Rightarrow \sin x=\sin \left(\pi-\frac{\pi x}{180}\right) \text { or } \sin \left(2 \pi+\frac{\pi x}{180}\right) \\
& x=\pi-\frac{\pi x}{180}, x=2 \pi+\frac{\pi x}{180} \\
& x=\frac{180 \pi}{180+\pi}, x=\frac{360 \pi}{180-\pi}
\end{aligned}$
On comparing, $m=360, n=180, p=180, q=180$
$\Rightarrow(m+n+p+q)=900$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
